channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Curated geopolitical news from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇦🇪🛢️ UAE Considered Leaving OPEC for Three Years Over Oil Strategy
item.description: A senior UAE presidential adviser revealed the country contemplated leaving OPEC for three years, believing the world is nearing the "autumn of the hydrocarbon age" and needs to maximize oil revenues while possible.

item.title: 🇺🇳🇺🇦 UN Refugee Agency Loses Million in Aid After Russian Strike on Ukraine Warehouse
item.description: A Russian missile struck a UN refugee agency warehouse in Dnipro, destroying $1 million worth of humanitarian aid destined for civilians in eastern Ukraine.

item.title: 🦠🌍 WHO Africa Head Warns Ebola Outbreak Risk Should Not Be Underestimated
item.description: The WHO regional director for Africa cautioned that just one case could spread the Ebola virus beyond the Democratic Republic of Congo and Uganda, urging heightened vigilance as no vaccine exists for the Bundibugyo strain.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 US Pauses $14 Billion Taiwan Arms Sale Citing Iran War Stockpile Needs
item.description: Taiwan's presidential office said it has not been notified of any changes to military sales, after a US official indicated a pause on a major arms package due to weapons stockpile demands from the Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇻🇪🇮🇳🛢️ Venezuelan Oil Emerges as India's Third-Largest Supplier Amid Hormuz Crisis
item.description: As the Iran war closes the Strait of Hormuz, Venezuela has become India's third-largest crude oil supplier this month, highlighting the global energy supply chain shifts caused by the conflict.

item.title: 🇸🇦🛢️ Saudi Oil Exports Surge 37% as Kingdom Bypasses Hormuz via Red Sea Route
item.description: Saudi Arabia reported a 37.4% surge in oil exports in March, the highest level in 3.5 years, as the East-West pipeline allowed crude shipments to bypass the Strait of Hormuz disruptions and reach global markets through the Red Sea port of Yanbu.

item.title: 🇦🇫⚖️ Taliban Decree Formalizes Child Marriage Rules, UN Expresses Grave Concern
item.description: The UN warned that a new Taliban decree on marriage separation implies child marriage is permitted, saying girls' silence at puberty can be interpreted as consent. The Afghan justice ministry defended the decree as following Islamic law.

item.title: 🇦🇪🤝 UAE Official Sees 50-50 Chance for Hormuz Deal, Warns Iran Against Overplaying Hand
item.description: UAE presidential adviser Anwar Gargash estimated only a 50-50 chance of a US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, warning Tehran against overestimating its leverage in negotiations as Gulf states face mounting economic pressure.

item.title: 🇵🇰🇮🇷 Pakistan Mediates US-Iran Peace Talks Amid Uranium and Hormuz Disputes
item.description: Iran foreign minister met with Pakistani officials to discuss proposals for ending the US-Iran war, as both nations remain at odds over uranium stockpile and Strait of Hormuz control.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳🇹🇼 US-China Thaw Plants Seeds for Taiwan Flashpoint
item.description: Trump says pending 14 billion dollar Taiwan arms deal is a very good negotiating chip with Beijing, even as Xi ramps up military and diplomatic pressure on Taipei.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇪🇺 NATO Allies Confused by Trump Sudden Shift on European Troop Deployments
item.description: NATO allies and defense officials expressed bewilderment at Trump announcement about potential US troop movements in Europe, creating uncertainty about American commitment to the alliance.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇺🇸 Taiwan denies receiving US military sales changes notification
item.description: Taiwan presidential office says no information about US adjusting military sales after acting US Navy Secretary suggests pause due to Iran war needs.

item.title: 🇧🇾🇷🇺 Belarus pledges not to join Ukraine war but will defend with Russia
item.description: President Lukashenko says Belarus will not be dragged into Ukraine conflict but will jointly defend against aggression with Russia after nuclear drills end.

item.title: 💥🇺🇦 Four killed in Ukrainian drone attack on Luhansk student dormitory
item.description: Russian officials report four dead and 35 children wounded in overnight Ukrainian drone strike on student dormitory in Russian-controlled Luhansk region.

item.title: 🇬🇧⚖️ UK police say Prince Andrew investigation will be lengthy
item.description: British police say investigation into King Charles brother Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor will be long and complex following arrest on misconduct allegations.

item.title: 🇮🇪⛪ Irish PM to discuss Middle East conflict with Pope
item.description: Taoiseach Micheal Martin will discuss ongoing Middle East conflict and reconciliation process in Ireland during meeting with Pope.

item.title: 🇺🇦 US approves $108 million HAWK missile system sale to Ukraine
item.description: The US State Department approved a $108 million sale of HAWK missile system equipment to Ukraine, including maintenance support, spare parts, and logistics assistance for the air defense systems.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇭🇺 Ukraine foreign minister meets new Hungarian counterpart on minority rights
item.description: Foreign Minister Andrii Sybiha held his first meeting with Hungary's new Foreign Minister Anita Orban in Sweden to discuss minority rights and bilateral relations between the two countries.

item.title: 🕋 Hajj pilgrimage overshadowed by Middle East war tensions
item.description: Over a million Muslims gathered in Mecca for the hajj pilgrimage under the shadow of the ongoing Middle East conflict, with Saudi officials attempting to keep the war from visitors' minds following Iranian attacks on Gulf targets.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Political Crisis Deepens as Opposition Vows Resistance
item.description: Turkeys opposition vowed to resist a court ruling that ousted its leader and annulled its congress, a move critics say aims to prolong President Erdogans 23-year rule.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Strikes Russian Oil Refinery 700 km from Border
item.description: President Zelenskiy announced Ukrainian forces attacked a Russian oil refinery in Yaroslavl, marking another long-range strike on Russian energy infrastructure as the war continues.

item.title: 🛢️ Oil Prices Rise as Investors Doubt US-Iran Breakthrough
item.description: Oil prices climbed as investors remained skeptical about prospects for a breakthrough in US-Iran peace talks, with Brent crude holding elevated amid Hormuz shipping disruptions.

item.title: 🇨🇳 China Cuts Oil Imports as Higher US Exports Wrongfoot Market
item.description: Chinese oil imports have fallen while US exports increased, catching market bulls off guard as physical crude prices hit all-time highs above $160 per barrel amid the Iran crisis.

item.title: 🇪🇺 Euro Zone Growth Set to Slow in 2026 as Middle East War Fuels Inflation
item.description: The European Commission warned that euro zone growth will slow in 2026 as the Middle East conflict triggers the second energy shock in less than five years.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Kremlin Denies China Trained Russian Soldiers for Ukraine War
item.description: The Kremlin dismissed as false a Reuters report that Chinas army secretly trained about 200 Russian soldiers in China late last year, some of whom went on to fight in Ukraine. The denial comes amid scrutiny of China-Russia military cooperation.
